name: Pickled Pigs' Ears (Tai Heo Ngam Chua)
recipeInstructions:
Lip-smacking, tangy, chewy, and exotic, these morsels go perfectly with drinks before dinner, and very well without drinks at any time.
1. Bring 2 quarts of water to a boil and add the alum. Boil for 5 minutes, then remove from heat and let cool.
2. In a separate pot, bring vinegar, sugar, and salt to a boil until the sugar is completely dissolved. Remove from heat and let cool.
3. Bring another 2 quarts of water to a boil. Drop in the pigs' ears and boil for 20 minutes. Remove the pigs' ears and slice them lengthwise into pieces about 1/4 inch wide.
4. Allow the sliced pigs' ears to cool, then soak in the alum water for 2 hours. Drain and rinse under cold water. Dry lightly.
5. Place the pigs' ears in a jar, pressing down. Pour in enough cooled vinegar mixture to completely cover the contents of the jar. Refrigerate.
NOTE: This can be eaten after 3 days and will keep for several weeks in the refrigerator.
